title: The Bracelet To Julia
content:
Why I tie about thy wrist,
Julia, this silken twist;
For what other reason 'tis
But to show thee how, in part,
Thou my pretty captive art?
But thy bond slave is my heart:
'tis but silk that bindeth thee,
Knap the thread and thou art free;
But 'tis otherwise with me:
I am bound and fast bound, so
That from thee I cannot go;
If I could, I would not so.
meaning:
Robert Herrick's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Bracelet To Julia"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
